headline: Anthropic's Mythos-class Claude Fable 5 dominates this cycle, with recommended educators framing it as a step change in vision and long-running agentic work.
body: Better Stack and Cyril Imhof lead with Claude Fable 5, the first Mythos-class model, citing top scores on SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code plus vision, long context, and extended memory for agent design in regulated industries [1][2]. Anthropic's own demo shows Fable 5 completing Pokemon FireRed from raw screenshots alone, where earlier models needed a helper harness [11], while AI Revolution notes its software, science, and cybersecurity reach paired with safety guardrails on high-risk domains [9]. Adjacent recommended coverage centers on agent infrastructure and efficiency: PAI builds a Claude Code Life OS with persistent memory and custom skills [0], Headroom compresses LLM inputs by 60-95 percent at zero token cost [4], and MiniMax M3 swaps full attention for sparse attention with KV cache and tiled I/O gains [5]. Outside the inner circle, Gemma 4's 31B model places fourth on the open model leaderboard while running on a single GPU [6], and Apple rebuilt Siri reportedly on Google Gemini [10].
